# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #686 | enhancement(scripts): archived projects strand refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/* forever — archive-time reap + keep-set legacy sweep (239 refs stranded) | open | — | Audit 2026-07-14: 239 refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/* refs stranded across ~42 archived projects — --drop-base is window-locked to pending (D-424-01), archive preserves baseline FILES but never reaps refs, and no sweep enumerates them; group barrier-base refs (dropped after pass) are at zero, confirming omission not design. Fix (M): archive-time reap of exactly the archived project's refs (fail-soft, never blocks finalize) + keep-set-disciplined legacy sweep mirroring the #680 orphan-baseline sweep; document the ref lifecycle; four-chain. Full body on GitHub #686. |
| #687 | enhancement(codex): subagent dispatch inherits the main session model and reasoning effort — reasoning/standard tier vocabulary retained as declarative prose | open | — | User directive 2026-07-14: current Codex cannot guarantee subagent quality below the main session's strength — every Codex subagent must inherit the main session model + reasoning effort. Keep reasoning/standard tier tokens everywhere (plan grammar, dispatch cards, wait budgets, profile prose) as declarative runtime-aligned metadata. Surfaces: TOML profile pins drop to inherit (prove via child-session probe), preflight/installer enforce + autofix the inherit posture, 3 Codex SKILL packs move to a single inheritance proof, REASONING_FLOOR_ROLES gets an explicit tested resolution, profile-parity + contract validators updated; Claude Code/opencode untouched; four-chain. Full body on GitHub #687. |
| #688 | enhancement(adaptive): fail-closed hardening of the #683 rebind-proof keyspace + ledger-status predicate (unreachable-today edges) | open | — | Four fail-closed defense-in-depth items surfaced by the #683 adversarial campaign, each provably unreachable through the CLI today (none is a live fail-open) but each a proof input broader than needed. (1) proveRebindAdmissible's `(ledgerStatuses \|\| {})` default degrades to pre-fix admit if the ledger is ever absent — make it refuse attribution (fail-closed default). (2) the R7 non-complete quantifier admits `n/a` (a selector-pruned gate that never re-reviews, not orphan-fenced) — restrict to `{pending,in_progress}`. (3) a node id named `__proto__` wedges fail-closed via the readLedgerStatuses plain-object key drop — add a reserved-name grammar refusal at freeze in plan-validator.js. (4) canonical-integer declared-path keys enumerate numeric-first, so a correct candidate_declared map can fail isCanonicalBlobMap and permanently wedge on review_journal_malformed — order-insensitive canonicalization in adaptive-schema.js or an integer-like-token grammar refusal. RED-first per item; adaptive-schema.js stays a 4-edition byte anchor; four-chain. Non-goals: no change to #683 rebind semantics or any live refusal. See docs/decisions/D-683-01.md. Full body on GitHub #688. |
| #689 | enhancement(scripts): fast/full/phase4-advance writeFileAtomic missing parent-dir fsync — same gap #685 fixed on the adaptive path | open | — | #685 added the parent-directory fsync to the adaptive path's writeFileAtomicReplace (adaptive-schema.js + roadmap.js). Three differently-named copies of the same tmp+fsync+rename algorithm carry the identical missing-parent-dir-fsync gap on the install-time opt-in fast/full six-phase paths: kaola-workflow-full-advance.js, kaola-workflow-fast-advance.js, kaola-workflow-phase4-advance.js (each `function writeFileAtomic`). Apply the same fix: fsync the parent directory after renameSync, platform fail-soft (never turn an accepted write into a refusal, never swallow a real rename/ENOSPC error). Non-vacuous regression per helper via the fs-singleton monkey-patch seam; cross-edition parity + chains for any edition-ported copies. See docs/decisions/D-683-01.md. Full body on GitHub #689. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
